action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 27's test_inspect.py showed the renderer returns a Tensor with white-ish corners (~0.945) while GT input corners are black (0,0,0). This background mismatch likely hurts LPIPS significantly. model.py in workspace is truncated/broken and needs a complete rewrite. Analysis: Before burning an eval iteration, I should locally test whether setting the renderer's bg_color to black improves LPIPS on validation objects. I will rewrite model.py with robust checkpoint loading and configurable bg_color, write a fast local_eval.py to compare white/black/None backgrounds on 20 val objects, and keep predict.py ready. Decision: In iteration 28, write clean model.py, local_eval.py, predict.py, and train.py. Run local_eval.py to measure LPIPS for each bg_color. Use the result to choose the best setting for the official eval in iteration 29.
